title: Support chat names, stability, and screen share
description:
Follow-up to the chat invite delivery fix. Three issues were reported after that release: message bubbles showed "User" instead of real names, customer chats sometimes closed on their own, and screen sharing / remote help stopped appearing during an active chat.

Display names now resolve first and last name from the users table (not just display_name/username). Each chat message includes senderDisplayName from the API. Active chat sync no longer drops the customer UI when the session tenant differs from the heartbeat tenant — it verifies the local session before clearing. Admin-initiated remote connection invites are shown even while a chat is open, and the Remote help toolbar button appears after the customer accepts a chat.

Who is affected: PipeShare support chat and remote assistance on the private OVH server (pipeshare.live). SaaS when the next platform version is applied in cPanel releases.
changeLog: Chat messages show sender first/last name instead of generic "User".
API enriches each message with senderDisplayName; lookup uses first_name and last_name.
Customer chat no longer auto-closes when session tenant differs from presence tenant.
Admin remote connection invites surface during an active support chat.
Remote help button appears after customer accepts a chat invite.
